sectiondef.header: Register offsets for the Tri-Mode Ethernet MAC. Each register is 32

description.para: The MAC is addressable through the Ethernet AVB Endpoint core.

sectiondef.header: Register offsets for the Ethernet Audio Video Endpoint. Each register
Packet base address offsets for the Ethernet Audio Video Endpoint Tx

description.para: Each PTP frames is stored in 256-byte chunks of BRAM. This BRAM can store 8 PTP frames of which only 6 are currently in use.

sectiondef.header: Base address offset for the Ethernet Audio Video Endpoint Rx

description.para: These PTP frames are stored in 256-byte chunks of BRAM. This BRAM can store 16 PTP frames.

briefdescription.para: Add some rails so that recovery is possible after a string of bad pDelay values.

detaileddescription.para: The RTC should be able to lock to within 100ppm of the slowest allowable clock (25 MHz). This equates to +/-4ps. Let's arbitrarily set the rails to 400ppm (+/-16ps) just in case someone decides to use a particularly bad oscillator. The lowest 20 bits of NewIncrement are fractions of a nanosecond, which equates to +/- 0x04189

sectiondef.header: AVB MAC MDIO register address space.
MDIO valid data mask (MDIO registers are all 16-bits).
MAC Statistic Counter names and CounterID.

briefdescription.para: This macro reads from the given AVB core register.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the device
is the register offset to be read

briefdescription.para: This macro writes to the given AVB core register.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the device
is the register offset to be written
is the 32-bit value to write to the register

briefdescription.para: This macro reads from the given PTP frame buffer.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the device
is the base address of the frame in the PTP BRAM packet buffer
is the offset address within the PTP frame

briefdescription.para: This macro writes to the given PTP frame buffer.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the device
is the base address of the frame in the PTP packet buffer
is the offset address within the PTP frame
is the 32-bit value to write to the register

briefdescription.para: This macro reads from the given TEMAC Configuration Register.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the device
is the register offset to be read

briefdescription.para: This macro writes to the given TEMAC Configuration Register.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the device
is the register offset to be written
is the 32-bit value to write to the register

briefdescription.para: This macro reads from the given MDIO Register using the TEMAC.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the device
is the Physical Address of the PHY
is the Address of the MDIO register within the addressed PHY

simplesect.para: The 32-bit value of the register (upper 16-bits will be all 0's)

briefdescription.para: This macro writes to the given MDIO Register using the TEMAC.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the device
is the Physical Address of the PHY
is the Address of the MDIO register within the addressed PHY
is the 32-bit value to write

briefdescription.para: This function reads the given Ethernet Statistic Register.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the device
is the Statistic Counter to be read
a pointer to the read value of the 64-bit value of the counter and it is updated by this function

simplesect.para: The CounterID's defined here are the same as the Statistic Counter Addresses as used with the TEMAC core. Since each counter is 8-bytes wide, we must multiply these Counter ID's by 8 (or a left shift of 3) when mapping this into the PLB memory map. The 64-bit counter value is read as two separate 32-bit accesses.

briefdescription.para: This function reads the current Real Time Counter (RTC) value.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the device
is a pointer to a struct in which to store the value read from the RTC (The RTC 48-bit seconds field and the 32-bit ns field of this struct are updated).

simplesect.para: This is provided as a basic function since the ns field MUST be read before the seconds/epoch registers (reading the ns samples the entire RTC in hardware).

briefdescription.para: This function writes to the Real Time Counter (RTC) Offset Registers.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the device
is the nanoseconds and seconds offset values that should be written to the RTC.

simplesect.para: This is provided as a basic function since the ns field MUST be written after the seconds/epoch offset registers (writing to the ns offset registers samples the entire RTC offset in hardware).

briefdescription.para: This header file contains the identifiers and basic driver functions (or macros) that can be used to access the device.

detaileddescription.para: Other driver functions are defined in xavb.h.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a mbr 09/19/08 First release 1.01a mbr 06/24/09 PTP frame format updates for IEEE802.1 AS draft 5-0 2_02a mbr 09/16/09 Updates for programmable PTP timers 2_04a kag 07/23/10 PTP frame format updates for IEEE802.1 AS draft 6-7 3_01a kag 08/29/11 Added new APIs to update the RX Filter Control Reg. Fix for CR:572539. Updated bit map for Rx Filter control reg. 3_01a asa 04/10/12 The AVB core is now brought inside the AxiEthernet core. Because of this there are changes in the register map.
